heading: Cluster Security
body:
Atlas manages database user creation. If the source cluster enforces authentication:

- Allow read access to the primary.

- If you want to use [mongorestore reference page](database-tools/mongorestore) with the
  [--oplogReplay](database-tools/mongorestore) option, you must delete
  the `admin` and `config` directories from the `dump` directory that
  [mongodump reference page](database-tools/mongodump) creates. The `admin` and `config`
  directories contain database user information that you can't add to
  an Atlas cluster with `mongorestore`. Use the mongorestore
  [--nsExclude](database-tools/mongorestore) to exclude the
  `admin.system.*` namespace.

You can't migrate any existing user or role information to Atlas.
For the destination Atlas cluster, create the appropriate database
users for supporting your application's usage patterns. Update your applications
as part of the cut-over procedure to use the new database users. To learn
more, see [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users).
